title: Senior Marketing Manager (m/w/d) – Nachhaltigkeitspartnerschaft
missing_skills: Sports sponsorship or brand activation in sports context
Near-native German proficiency (candidate is B1+, role requires Muttersprachenniveau)
Stadium or live event activation experience
Partnership management with external sports/media organizations
Content campaign creation for consumer-facing brand (not B2B product)
core_responsibilities: Lead activation and execution of strategic football partnership with Borussia Mönchengladbach
Conceive and implement brand and marketing measures within the sustainability partnership
Develop ongoing content campaigns and product marketing assets
Manage stadium activations and exclusive sales/customer events
Steer external agencies, monitor campaigns, and deliver performance reports
recommended_emphasis: Highlight any event operations and trade fair management as proof of live activation experience
Emphasize cross-functional stakeholder management and C-level reporting to show organizational maturity
Showcase content/campaign development work from product launches to align with content campaign responsibility
Position GTM strategy experience as transferable to partnership activation planning
Mention any sustainability-related projects or values to align with the Nachhaltigkeitspartnerschaft theme
risk_flags: German language gap is critical: role requires near-native level (Muttersprachenniveau bevorzugt), candidate is B1+ — likely disqualifying
No sports sponsorship or brand activation in sports/entertainment context — core domain mismatch
Role is B2C brand-focused (football partnership, stadium events); candidate background is B2B product marketing
No evidence of agency stewardship or sponsorship asset management in CV
summary: This role centers on activating a high-profile sports sponsorship with Borussia Mönchengladbach, requiring near-native German and proven brand/sponsorship activation experience — two areas where the candidate has significant gaps. While the candidate brings solid B2B marketing, GTM strategy, and event operations skills, the domain mismatch (sports sponsorship vs. B2B product marketing) and the German language requirement make this a low-fit opportunity.
